issues: The draft headline 'Moncton Hawks, Winnipeg Jets Explore Long-Term Arrangement' does not appear verbatim in the OCR — the actual visible headline from the page is 'Lafleur celebrates with first goal' and 'shorthanded goal lifts Jets', which are adjacent-column noise. The Sockeyes-relevant article (the Hawks/Jets piece) has no clear headline visible in the OCR extract.
The draft mentions 'Bob Essansa' — the OCR spells this name 'Bob Essensa' (correct NHL spelling: Bob Essensa).

The draft states nine players were assigned to Moncton, but the OCR lists nine players by name: Ron Wilson, Brent Hughes, Guy Larose, Len Nielsen, Todd Flichel, Matt Hervey, Jamie Husgen, Stephane Beauregard, and Bob Essensa — the draft omits Brent Hughes, Guy Larose, Len Nielsen, Todd Flichel, and Jamie Husgen while incorrectly summarising the list.

The draft describes the article as being about a 'franchise arrangement' exploration, but the OCR makes clear a three-year contract already exists and the question is whether the option year will be exercised and renegotiated — the framing is subtly inaccurate.
Matt Hervey is a known 1987 Sockeyes player; his assignment to Moncton is the primary Sockeyes-relevant detail and should be foregrounded in the description.
review.correctedDescriptionShort: Times-Transcript article on the Winnipeg Jets–Moncton Hawks AHL arrangement for 1988-89, noting that ex-Sockeye Matt Hervey was among nine players assigned to Moncton after a Jets exhibition game against Minnesota.
review.correctedDescriptionLong: The Times-Transcript reports on the uncertain future of the Winnipeg Jets' AHL affiliation with the Moncton Hawks as the 1988-89 season begins. Jets GM John Ferguson says he wants a long-term deal, but Hawks ownership is cautious after the previous season's poor on-ice performance and attendance drop. Coach Rick Bowness pledges a more competitive roster. Notably, nine players were assigned to Moncton following Winnipeg's exhibition game against Minnesota, including ex-Richmond Sockeyes player Matt Hervey, along with Ron Wilson, Brent Hughes, Guy Larose, Len Nielsen, Todd Flichel, Jamie Husgen, and goaltenders Stephane Beauregard and Bob Essensa.
review.notes: No clear standalone headline is visible for the Hawks/Jets article in the OCR — the headlines visible ('Lafleur celebrates with first goal', 'shorthanded goal lifts Jets') belong to adjacent-column stories. The corrected headline field has been left blank accordingly. Editor should check the original scan for any headline above the Hawks piece. Bob Essensa is the correct spelling (future Jets NHL goaltender).
